course.title: Build and Ship an Authorized Web Crawler and Accessible Site Auditor with Python 3.14
course.description: Python, HTTP, web, data-structure, and Git learners who need to design, build, test, operate, and defend an authorized bounded crawler and accessible site-quality auditor instead of copy a brittle requests-and-selector script. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate python 3.14.6; authorization, ethics, privacy, and legal-review routing; reproducible environments; bounded crawl scope; url resolution and conservative identity; rfc 9309 robots and sitemaps; httpx retrieval and prerequisite http reuse; response bytes and encoding; whatwg-informed html parsing and parser differentials; beautiful soup 4.15 and lxml 6.1; css selectors and typed extraction; provenance; link discovery; crawl graphs and frontier state; url traps; per-origin politeness; structured asyncio concurrency; authenticated-session boundaries; incremental validators and checkpoints; exact and near duplicate evidence; search indexing, canonical, hreflang, json-ld, and social metadata; wcag-informed accessibility auditing with human review; resource and performance evidence; playwright 1.61 authorized dynamic rendering; block and captcha stop policy with no evasion; ssrf and untrusted-content defense; csv, json lines, and accessible reports; deterministic fixtures, local servers, property and mutation evidence; scrapy 2.17 architecture and autothrottle; packaging, scheduling, operations, migration, rollback, recovery, and production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-python evidence using original fixed fixtures plus explicit dns, network, http library, parser, filesystem, browser, target-owner, accessibility, privacy, security, legal-review, load, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative authentic crawler and site-audit deliveries and a performance-based unfamiliar-site defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves stakeholder outcome, authorization, revision, Python and dependency versions, seed and origin, URL and scope policy, robots decision, response and parser identity, extraction provenance, crawl state, privacy and accessibility requirement, bounded work, changed-case test, failure, recovery, and explicit browser-versus-controlled-system limits before adding one crawler boundary.

Browser Python uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure functions. It does not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; those require controlled evidence.

Passing work requires stable scenario and artifact identity, prediction, intermediate URL, response, tree, field, graph, or state evidence, exact observable output, a changed and rejected case, a test that detects a deliberate defect, accessible stakeholder evidence, and a named owner for remaining risk.

credential.title: Build and Ship an Authorized Web Crawler and Accessible Site Auditor with Python 3.14 Certificate

modules.title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Authorization, Ethics, Privacy, and Evidence
modules.description: A team says “scrape this public site” without a site owner, permitted purpose, data policy, stop contact, accessibility outcome, or proof that the result helps anyone. Learners cumulatively produce authorized stakeholder and evidence charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define the stakeholder decision, users, page population, evidence, success, failure, review cadence, and observable value before choosing crawler technology.
Record who controls the target, what access is authorized, which identities and paths are permitted, and who can pause or revoke the crawl.
Separate technical policy from terms, contracts, copyright, privacy, and jurisdiction questions and route uncertain cases to qualified review.

modules.title: Repository, Python Runtime, Dependencies, Parsers, and Reproducibility
modules.description: The crawler runs from an unknown revision with floating packages, an undocumented parser, no license inventory, and no clean-environment proof. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible crawler repository baseline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inspect revision, status, existing behavior, tests, configuration, data fixtures, licenses, secrets, and a recovery anchor before editing.
Record Python implementation and version, platform, SSL backend, event-loop assumptions, encoding environment, and supported runtime matrix.
Pin direct and resolved dependencies, verify hashes and sources, review licenses and advisories, and prove clean installation.

modules.title: Seeds, Origins, Paths, Query Space, Budgets, and Stop Policy
modules.description: A seed expands to subdomains, downloads, calendars, search results, and logout links while the crawler has no page, byte, time, or incident limit. Learners cumulatively produce bounded crawl-scope policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Record each seed URL, stakeholder, purpose, authorization source, expected content class, and crawl revision.
Define allowed schemes, normalized hosts, ports, subdomains, redirects, and cross-origin resources with deny-by-default rules.
Express permitted paths, methods, query keys, values, pagination, fragments, and content types as testable admission rules.

modules.title: URL References, Resolution, Identity Keys, Unicode, and Canonicalization
modules.description: Relative references, fragments, default ports, dot segments, percent encodings, Unicode hosts, and tracking parameters produce duplicates or escape scope. Learners cumulatively produce URL resolution and identity pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Parse scheme, authority, userinfo, host, port, path, query, and fragment without treating a URL as a trustworthy string.
Resolve relative, scheme-relative, path-relative, query-only, and fragment-only references against the effective document base.
Remove fragments from network-fetch identity while preserving them when the stakeholder report needs document-location evidence.

modules.title: Robots Exclusion Protocol, Failure Semantics, Caching, and Sitemaps
modules.description: The crawler fetches robots.txt from the wrong path, merges groups incorrectly, treats an outage as permission, obeys nonstandard delay blindly, and trusts cross-host sitemap URLs. Learners cumulatively produce RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Fetch `/robots.txt` at the correct service origin and use an honest stable product token and contact identity.
Apply RFC 9309 group selection, rule matching, percent encoding, longest-match precedence, and allow-over-disallow tie behavior.
Handle successful, unavailable, unreachable, redirect, oversized, and stale robots responses with explicit cache and conservative stop policy.

modules.title: HTTP Client Reuse, Retrieval, Redirects, Validators, and Bounded Bodies
modules.description: Each page creates a new client, sends browser-like lies, follows redirects outside scope, reads unlimited bodies, and accepts an HTML-looking error page. Learners cumulatively produce bounded HTTP page-fetch contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Reuse one configured client or bounded pool and close it deliberately so connection, proxy, TLS, cookie, and resource ownership are visible.
Use safe retrieval methods and an honest User-Agent, Accept, Accept-Encoding, conditional, and contact policy without impersonation.
Classify transport, status, media type, length, content coding, and domain outcomes before parsing the representation.

modules.title: Response Bytes, Content Codings, Character Encodings, and Source Identity
modules.description: The parser receives truncated compressed bytes, guesses the platform encoding, ignores a conflicting declaration, and hashes normalized text as if it were the source. Learners cumulatively produce byte-to-Unicode admission ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Track wire, compressed, decompressed, decoded, and retained sizes and stop before an expansion or memory budget is exceeded.
Validate and apply declared content codings in order while distinguishing unsupported, corrupt, partial, and over-budget representations.
Record transport declarations, byte-order marks, HTML declarations, parser detection, replacement behavior, and the final chosen encoding.

modules.title: HTML Tokens, Error Recovery, Parser Selection, and Differential Evidence
modules.description: A selector works under one parser but fails under another because omitted elements, foster parenting, broken nesting, and script or noscript state produce different trees. Learners cumulatively produce bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish source bytes, decoded text, tokens, parse errors, the constructed tree, serialized markup, and browser DOM state.
Predict omitted tags, mismatched nesting, duplicate attributes, character references, templates, foreign content, and recovery effects on target nodes.
Select html.parser, lxml HTML, lxml XML, or html5lib deliberately and pin that parser in code, tests, and metadata.

modules.title: CSS Selectors, Tree Navigation, Cardinality, Text, and Attributes
modules.description: A brittle absolute selector returns the wrong repeated card, missing attributes become strings, nested text collapses, and a parser upgrade silently changes cardinality. Learners cumulatively produce selector and extraction contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use type, class, ID, attribute, combinator, grouping, and supported pseudo-class selectors with explicit scope and library support.
State zero, one, optional, at-least-one, and many cardinality expectations and reject ambiguous or duplicate matches.
Anchor extraction at a stable semantic component and resolve fields relative to it instead of coupling to page-wide positions.

modules.title: Typed Records, Missingness, Provenance, Validation, and Rejection
modules.description: The scraper emits loose dictionaries, converts missing prices to zero, drops source locations, and keeps partly invalid rows because most fields look useful. Learners cumulatively produce typed provenance-preserving extraction record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model raw, parsed, normalized, validated, and exported fields with dataclasses or typed records and a versioned schema.
Keep missing, empty, unknown, redacted, malformed, not-applicable, and zero values distinct through validation and export.
Attach source URL, response identity, selector or rule, node evidence, retrieval time, parser, and transformation history to decisions.

modules.title: Link-Bearing Elements, Base URLs, Relations, Discovery, and Scheduling
modules.description: The crawler follows canonical, alternate, preload, form, script, mailto, and base-altered references as if every discovered URL were a navigational page. Learners cumulatively produce typed link-discovery record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Extract navigational, embedded, form, metadata, refresh, and structured-data URLs by element and attribute with distinct policies.
Apply effective response URL and the first valid HTML base element deliberately and retain both source and resolved references.
Interpret rel tokens such as canonical, alternate, next, prev, nofollow, sponsored, and ugc as evidence or hints, not universal commands.

modules.title: Crawl Graphs, Frontier Strategy, Visited State, Depth, and Determinism
modules.description: URLs are marked visited after download, duplicates race into the queue, depth changes with concurrency, and restart cannot explain which parent discovered a page. Learners cumulatively produce deterministic crawl-frontier state machine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent pages, fetch identities, redirect edges, discovery edges, canonical hints, embedded resources, and rejection states separately.
Choose FIFO, depth-first, priority, sitemap, recrawl, or hybrid ordering from stakeholder value and fairness requirements.
Reserve an admitted URL atomically before scheduling and reconcile queued, active, completed, redirected, rejected, and retry states.

modules.title: Facets, Calendars, Sessions, Pagination, Search, and Infinite URL Spaces
modules.description: A finite site generates unbounded filter permutations, dates, tracking IDs, sort orders, empty searches, and repeated pagination paths. Learners cumulatively produce URL-space trap detector and budget policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Detect repeated filter dimensions, permutations, empty states, sort variants, and low-value combinations using explicit site knowledge.
Bound date ranges, next and previous traversal, generated archives, and cyclic calendar links against stakeholder time scope.
Identify session, tracking, nonce, cache-buster, and personalized parameters without deleting unknown keys by guesswork.

modules.title: Per-Origin Politeness, Concurrency, Retry Classification, and Adaptive Rate
modules.description: A global sleep still sends bursts per host, retries permanent blocks, ignores Retry-After, synchronizes workers, and increases load after slow responses. Learners cumulatively produce per-origin politeness and retry controller through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Schedule requests by effective origin with minimum start spacing, stable crawler identity, robots and owner policy, and monotonic time.
Limit active work per origin and globally while accounting for redirects, browser resources, retries, and queued fairness.
Retry only transient, safe, replayable failures under an attempt and elapsed-time budget and stop on denial or policy signals.

modules.title: Asyncio Task Groups, Backpressure, Queues, Cancellation, and Cleanup
modules.description: Fire-and-forget tasks outlive the crawl, an unbounded queue grows, workers swallow cancellation, results depend on race order, and the client leaks. Learners cumulatively produce structured concurrent crawl engine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Own related crawl tasks in TaskGroup scopes and define how one failure cancels, aggregates, or isolates sibling work.
Bound frontier and result queues, state who can close them, and make producers wait or shed work under explicit policy.
Acquire global, origin, browser, and output capacity in one documented order and release every permit in finally paths.

modules.title: Cookies, Authentication, Private Data, Write Avoidance, and Session Cleanup
modules.description: A crawler logs in with a personal account, stores cookies in logs, follows logout and mutation links, and exports fields beyond the authorized purpose. Learners cumulatively produce authorized session and data-custody contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use a dedicated cookie jar, inspect domain, path, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, partition, expiry, and redirect scope, and avoid ambient sharing.
Use dedicated least-privilege identities, approved secret sources, documented pages and fields, rotation, revocation, and audit ownership.
Restrict crawler navigation to safe retrieval and block logout, unsubscribe, cart, form, API mutation, and side-effecting endpoints.

modules.title: Validators, Incremental Crawls, Checkpoints, Resume, and Deletion
modules.description: Every run downloads everything, a 304 loses prior content, checkpoints duplicate rows after crash, and disappeared pages remain reported as current. Learners cumulatively produce incremental crawl and recovery ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Store ETag and Last-Modified with representation identity and issue correct conditional requests without treating validators as content hashes.
On 304, reconnect current response metadata to a compatible retained representation and reject missing or mismatched cache state.
Checkpoint frontier, visited states, response identity, output transaction, schema, and revision atomically or through a replayable log.

modules.title: Exact Duplicates, Boilerplate, Similarity, Canonicals, and Change Detection
modules.description: Tracking variants duplicate pages, shared navigation dominates hashes, a canonical hint suppresses evidence, and a similarity score is treated as proof of sameness. Learners cumulatively produce content identity and change-evidence model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep response-byte, decoded-source, normalized-text, extracted-record, and report hashes distinct and version their transformations.
Separate repeated navigation, templates, legal text, and page-specific main content with site-tested rules and retained raw evidence.
Use documented tokenization, fingerprints, thresholds, and labeled evaluation and report uncertain similarity without claiming semantic equality.

modules.title: Search Metadata, Indexing Directives, Canonical Signals, and Language Alternates
modules.description: The audit equates crawl permission with indexability, trusts one canonical tag, ignores HTTP robots fields, and reports hreflang without reciprocal or language checks. Learners cumulatively produce search discovery and indexability audit record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Extract title and description candidates with cardinality, location, text quality, changed-case, and stakeholder-specific non-ranking claims.
Reconcile redirects, HTTP canonical links, HTML canonical links, sitemap entries, internal links, and content identity without forcing agreement.
Parse page-level robots meta and X-Robots-Tag directives by applicable user agent and separate crawling from indexing and serving.

modules.title: JSON-LD, Structured Data, Open Graph, Social Metadata, and Non-Guarantees
modules.description: Malformed JSON-LD is silently accepted, remote contexts are fetched, structured claims conflict with visible content, and complete metadata is reported as a guaranteed rich result. Learners cumulatively produce structured and social metadata evidence report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Locate JSON-LD scripts, enforce byte and depth limits, parse without fetching remote contexts, preserve duplicates, and surface malformed documents.
Validate context, graph, type, identifier, required properties, arrays, language, URLs, and supported vocabulary against a versioned policy.
Compare structured claims with visible page evidence and flag missing, contradictory, hidden, stale, or unverifiable values.

modules.title: Accessible Names, Images, Language, Headings, Landmarks, and Human Review
modules.description: The tool counts alt attributes, assumes heading rank alone proves structure, ignores accessible names and language, and labels automated checks WCAG conformance. Learners cumulatively produce accessible page-quality audit with human-review handoff through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Check declared page and part language, document title presence and purpose, and conflicts with observed content while retaining human-review limits.
Extract headings, labels, sections, and landmarks in document context and flag missing or misleading structure without enforcing a simplistic numeric ladder.
Classify informative, decorative, functional, complex, grouped, linked, and text images and assess alternative evidence in context.

modules.title: Timing, Resource Graphs, Transfer, Caching, and User-Performance Boundaries
modules.description: The audit calls server response time page speed, double-counts cached resources, ignores compression and render work, and reports one lab run as user experience. Learners cumulatively produce bounded page-resource and timing audit through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate queue, DNS, connect, TLS, request, first-byte, body, parse, render, and total timing with clock and instrumentation limits.
Classify document, stylesheet, script, image, font, media, API, iframe, preload, and redirect resources with initiator and origin.
Reconcile compressed transfer, decoded body, cached reuse, duplicate URLs, variant keys, and large-resource policy.

modules.title: Dynamic Rendering Need, Browser Contexts, Network Budgets, Readiness, and Cleanup
modules.description: Every page launches a browser, waits for networkidle forever, loads third-party resources, shares cookies, and extracts an unstable animation state. Learners cumulatively produce authorized bounded Playwright render adapter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compare server HTML, embedded data, public API, progressive enhancement, and browser output and render only when the stakeholder evidence requires it.
Use isolated nonpersistent contexts with explicit viewport, locale, timezone, permissions, service-worker, storage, and credential policy.
Route, observe, allow, block, and count document and subresource requests by type, origin, bytes, redirects, and owner policy.

modules.title: Blocks, CAPTCHAs, Rate Signals, Identity, and No-Evasion Boundary
modules.description: A 200 challenge page is parsed as content, the team rotates identities, solves CAPTCHAs, hides automation, and treats owner defenses as an engineering obstacle. Learners cumulatively produce block detection and owner-escalation policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Detect denial, challenge, login, consent, interstitial, throttling, suspicious redirects, and semantic block pages from multiple signals.
Treat CAPTCHA and human-verification challenges as stop and owner-coordination signals rather than puzzles to automate around.
Use an honest stable identity and refuse proxy rotation, fingerprint spoofing, account cycling, stealth plugins, or other defense evasion.

modules.title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Redirects, Parser Limits, and Output Injection
modules.description: User-provided seeds reach local services, DNS changes after validation, redirects escape the allowlist, XML resolves entities, and spreadsheet formulas execute from exported cells. Learners cumulatively produce untrusted-input security boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Allowlist schemes and destinations, reject credentials and dangerous address classes, constrain ports, and separate user input from fetch authority.
Resolve and classify every connection destination at the correct time, bind policy to the connected peer where possible, and recheck changes.
Apply full destination, credential, DNS, port, method, and budget policy on every redirect and browser subrequest.

modules.title: CSV, JSON Lines, Schemas, Stable Output, Redaction, and Accessible Reports
modules.description: Columns reorder between runs, newlines corrupt CSV, None becomes ambiguous, JSON arrays cannot stream, secrets enter logs, and the visual report has no table semantics. Learners cumulatively produce versioned machine and accessible human report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define UTF-8, newline handling, dialect, ordered fields, quoting, formula policy, missing values, limits, and atomic replacement.
Emit one bounded versioned JSON record per line with stable types, Unicode policy, provenance, and explicit invalid-record handling.
Sort by documented semantic keys and stable tie-breakers so concurrency, retries, and resume do not change comparable output.

modules.title: HTML Fixtures, Fake Transports, Local Servers, Properties, Faults, and Mutation
modules.description: Tests hit live websites, fixtures contain one perfect page, retries never fail midway, URL properties are untested, and snapshots pass after a broken selector returns nothing. Learners cumulatively produce layered deterministic crawler verification suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build minimal, representative, malformed, Unicode, oversized, dynamic, accessibility, and changed-site fixtures with exact purpose labels.
Use fake transports for deterministic units and a controlled local server for redirect, streaming, timeout, encoding, cache, and connection evidence.
Test URL resolution, idempotent canonical keys, scope monotonicity, fragment behavior, query policies, and adversarial address forms as properties.

modules.title: Scrapy Architecture, Scheduler, Middleware, AutoThrottle, and Observability
modules.description: A hand-built crawler grows hidden hooks, middleware order changes behavior, duplicate filters lose identity, AutoThrottle is treated as permission, and metrics cannot reconstruct failures. Learners cumulatively produce Scrapy 2.17 scale and observability transfer plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace engine, scheduler, downloader, spider, item pipeline, signals, extensions, requests, responses, failures, and ownership in Scrapy.
Map canonical request identity, priorities, persistence, depth, retries, duplicate filtering, and resume semantics into scheduler evidence.
Reason about request and response traversal order, built-in middleware, exceptions, short-circuiting, and settings merge before customization.

modules.title: CLI, Configuration, Packaging, Scheduling, Overlap, Logs, and Recovery
modules.description: The script embeds targets and secrets, has ambiguous exit status, runs overlapping cron jobs, writes partial reports, logs content forever, and cannot restore a prior release. Learners cumulatively produce installable and operable crawler release candidate through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Design typed CLI and configuration precedence for targets, policy, budgets, output, dry-run, checkpoint, resume, revision, and secret references.
Build an import-safe package with declared dependencies, console entry point, resources, metadata, license, and reproducible artifact identity.
Install the immutable artifact into a clean environment and run help, fixture crawl, controlled local crawl, output validation, and clean shutdown.

modules.title: Release Gates, Capacity, Compliance, Migration, Rollback, and Defense
modules.description: The candidate passes fixture tests but lacks owner signoff, load evidence, privacy review, parser migration proof, rollback rehearsal, residual-risk ownership, and support documentation. Learners cumulatively produce production crawler evidence and recovery defense through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Reconcile authorization, scope, robots, HTTP, URL, parse, extraction, crawl graph, metadata, accessibility, output, and changed-case evidence.
Measure pages, resources, bytes, parse time, memory, queue, concurrency, browser load, output, cancellation, and recovery at representative scale.
Obtain accountable owner, privacy, security, accessibility, data, legal-review, operations, and target-site approvals with expiry and revocation paths.
